trivia_bullets.text: Viscera Seer originated in Magic 2011 (M11, 2010). It has been reprinted extensively: Commander 2013, The List, a Magic Online Promo (2020), Commander Legends (two printings: no. 158 and the etched foil no. 658), The Lost Caverns of Ixalan Commander (2023), Tarkir: Dragonstorm Commander (2025), and Secrets of Strixhaven Commander (2026). The depth of reprint history reflects sustained Commander and Pauper demand. [Scryfall]

A one-mana free (no-mana-cost activation) sacrifice outlet that replaces each sacrifice with a Scry 1 is considered one of the most efficient sacrifice enablers at common. Viscera Seer appears in over 292 recorded combo lines on MTG Decks, including the Mikaeus, the Unhallowed + persist creature infinite-scry loop found in tens of thousands of EDH decks. It is legal in Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Pauper, Commander, and Paupercommander. [Scryfall; WebSearch: MTG Decks Viscera Seer combos snippet]
sources: WebSearch: MTG Decks combo count via search snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "In matters of life and death, he trusts his gut." No speaker attributed. The pun — 'trusts his gut' as both viscera-reading divination and instinct — is the card's entire personality. [Scryfall]
